name: Anu Supreme
description: Anu, also known as An, was the supreme sky god in ancient Sumerian religion and mythology. He was considered the father of the gods and the lord of the heavens, embodying ultimate authority and kingship. Originating from the primordial cosmic sea, Anu represented the distant, unattainable, and abstract aspects of divinity. While not actively involved in the daily affairs of mortals, his decrees and judgments held immense power. He resided in the highest heaven, attended by other deities and celestial beings. Anu's power was so great that even the other gods sought his approval. He was the source of legitimate rule and bestowed kingship upon mortal rulers. Anu's symbols often represented his celestial nature and his association with kingship. Although less frequently depicted in anthropomorphic form than other Sumerian gods, his influence permeated all aspects of Sumerian life, solidifying his position as the ultimate sovereign of the cosmos. His legacy continued to resonate through later Mesopotamian civilizations. He was considered the ultimate source of law and order in the universe. His disinterest in direct intervention led to other deities taking on more active roles in human affairs, but his overarching authority remained unchallenged. Anu's role was to maintain cosmic order and enforce divine law, ensuring the proper functioning of the universe.
subtitle: Supreme Sky God and Father of Gods

keyMyths.title: Anu and Kingship
keyMyths.description: Anu bestows kingship upon mortal rulers, granting them the divine right to govern. This act legitimizes their power and connects them to the celestial realm.

keyMyths.title: The Descent of Ishtar to the Underworld
keyMyths.description: Ishtar seeks permission from Anu to descend to the underworld. This illustrates Anu's supreme authority, even over powerful goddesses.

keyMyths.title: Anu and the Creation Myth
keyMyths.description: In some creation myths, Anu plays a role as the distant, primordial force from which the universe and other gods emerged. He represents the initial state of cosmic existence.

sources.author: Samuel Noah Kramer
sources.work: The Sumerians: Their History, Culture, and Character
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Sumerian civilization, including religious beliefs and mythology.
sources.text: A comprehensive overview of Sumerian civilization, including religious beliefs and mythology.
